market.name: ASML Holding N.V. - New York Re

buys.title: 2 Stocks the Entire AI Market Depends On !!
buys.reason: ASML is a Dutch company that manufactures lithography systems, the machines that project circuit patterns onto silicon wafers to create semiconductors. Its extreme ultraviolet (EUV) machines are the only tools on Earth capable of printing today's most advanced chip designs, and no other company makes them. TSMC, Samsung, Intel, and every leading edge chip maker buy from ASML because there is no alternative. EUV is how 3 nanometer chips and 2 nanometer chips exist, and ASML's next generation high NA EUV platform is how the nodes beyond that will be printed. As chips become more complex, more EUV exposures are required per chip, and AI is accelerating this litho intensity even faster than any previous technology cycle. The CEO updated 2026 guidance, increasing the expected revenue range to between 36 billion and 40 billion euros while maintaining gross margin expectations of 51 to 53%. ASML is currently under-shipping demand and cannot build EUV machines fast enough to meet customer demand. By 2027, ASML aims to ship at least 80 tools (up from 44 in the prior year), effectively doubling the chip-making capacity it delivers through EUV alone. Additionally, AI-grade DRAM adoption is accelerating—DRAM now accounts for 51% of net sales systems (up from 30% in Q4 2025), representing a durable new dimension to ASML's growth driven by AI accelerators requiring high bandwidth memory. Once memory makers commit to EUV intensive nodes, they keep buying tools for years, making this a sticky revenue stream rather than a one-time event.
buys.cautions: Primary risk is regulatory. ASML is already prohibited from selling its most advanced EUV chips to China, and the rules governing what it can sell and to whom are still evolving. China currently accounts for 20% of ASML's revenue, and any further tightening of restrictions or extensions of controls to additional product lines could trim that contribution further. Management acknowledged this directly, noting that 2026 revenue guidance range is set specifically to accommodate potential outcomes of ongoing discussions around export control. For a company with a hardware monopoly on the world's most critical technology, the risk isn't competition, it's policy, and the regulatory environment remains live and unpredictable.
